title: Analysis: Assessing Inflation’s Impact
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Inflation raises living costs, influences interest rates, and affects investment decisions, while persistent high inflation continues to challenge consumers and
excerpt: Inflation raises living costs, influences interest rates, and affects investment decisions, while persistent high inflation continues to challenge consumers and policymakers. Energy and shelter remain major inflation drivers, and protection strategies are crucial for portfolios.

content.introduction: Inflation raises living costs, influences interest rates, and affects investment decisions, while persistent high inflation continues to challenge consumers and policymakers. Energy and shelter remain major inflation drivers, and protection strategies are crucial for portfolios.
body: Current inflation affects the [economy](https://www.usbank.com/investing/financial-perspectives/market-news/economic-recovery-status.html) by raising the cost of living, influencing interest rates, and shaping investment decisions. [1](#1 "Footnote 1") Investors also watch the Bureau of Economic Analysis’ Personal Consumption Expenditure price index because the [Federal Reserve](https://www.usbank.com/investing/financial-perspectives/market-news/federal-reserve-tapering-asset-purchases.html) uses it in defining its inflation target, and February’s PCE price index rose 2.8% from a year earlier. [2](#2 "Footnote 2") More recently, investors have focused on what comes next as oil transportation constraints in the [Middle East](https://www.usbank.com/investing/financial-perspectives/market-news/us-stock-market-volatility-continues.html) push energy prices higher and raise concern about future inflation. Even so, inflation remains high enough that [interest rate](https://www.usbank.com/investing/financial-perspectives/market-news/federal-reserve-interest-rate.html) expectations can change quickly as new data arrives. Energy drove most of March’s jump in prices, but [shelter](https://www.usbank.com/investing/financial-perspectives/investing-insights/interest-rates-impact-on-housing-market.html) remains a major source of ongoing pressure. Inflation remains a key driver of interest rates and [market volatility](https://www.usbank.com/investing/financial-perspectives/market-news/is-a-market-correction-coming.html), and tariffs and energy shocks can create short-term setbacks even when the longer-term trend is improving.
While the pace of rising inflation is slowing, persistently higher prices continue to weigh on consumers and policymakers alike.

Data Source: Statbel Contribution of Energy and Food to CPI YoY Belgium SQUARE MANAGEMENT > High Inflation Analysis and its Impact on Private Banking 5 HOW TO PROTECT PORTFOLIOS AGAINST INFLATION The last ten years have been characterised by an environment of low inflation and low or negative interest rates in Western countries and to some extent in emerging Asia.
